behavior_summary: BENCHEW::look: When the bench item is examined (U8P_BENCHEW::look event), the handler creates the literal string "bench ", asks the item to 'bark' that string (display/speak its short description), frees the string, inspects the bark return value briefly, then yields/suspends. This is a short UI/feedback handler with no global side-effects.

BENCHEW::gotHit: When the item receives a hit event, the handler examines the second argument (a signed/word parameter). If a particular condition on that argument is satisfied (the exact test is inferred from bytecode tests), the item’s visual frame is changed to frame index 1 via Item::setFrame; otherwise no action is taken. The first argument is present but not used in the observed sequence.
notable_control_flow: U8P_BENCHEW::BENCHEW::look performs a simple call sequence: allocate/push string, call external Item::bark, free the string, push/pop the bark return value, then suspend/yield before returning — indicating a U8P_BENCHEW::look/inspect interaction that yields to the engine.

U8P_BENCHEW::BENCHEW::gotHit contains a conditional branch based on the second parameter (arg2). On match it performs a single external call Item::setFrame(item, 1); otherwise it returns without side effects.
No global flags are read or written by either handler in the provided excerpt; effects are localized to the item instance and its visual state or to transient UI output.
open_questions: What exact predicate is used in the gotHit test on arg2 (the disassembled conditional's precise semantics are inferred but not explicit)?
What is the meaning of the first gotHit parameter (arg1) — it is present but unused in the observed code; does it convey damage source/type elsewhere?

What does the bark routine return and how (if at all) should the return value influence subsequent logic — the U8P_BENCHEW::look handler inspects it briefly before suspending but takes no visible further action.
Are there other event handlers (e.g., enterFastArea/leaveFastArea) for this item in the full script not included in the excerpt that alter behavior or global flags?

main_function.name: U8P_BENCHEW::look
main_function.purpose: Provide a short descriptive response when the bench is examined: produce the string "bench " and invoke the item's bark/display routine to show or speak that text, then yield.
entry_conditions: Event 0 / 'U8P_BENCHEW::look' (triggered when an actor/player inspects or looks at the bench item).

function_map.name: U8P_BENCHEW::look
function_map.role: Handle the 'U8P_BENCHEW::look' (examine) event for the bench: produce/display the bench's short description via Item::bark.

function_map.name: U8P_BENCHEW::gotHit
function_map.role: Handle the 'gotHit' event: inspect hit parameters and set the item's frame to 1 when a specific condition on the second parameter holds.

function_map.name: Item::bark
function_map.role: Engine routine used to display/speak short text from an item/process to a target/process/player.

function_map.name: Item::setFrame
function_map.role: Engine routine to change an item's visual/animation frame index.

external_calls.purpose: Present the static string "bench " to the viewer/process (display or speak the item name/description).
Change the item's visual/animation frame to index 1 when the inferred hit condition is met.
